title: hieracium rigidum subsp. insuetum
intro: Hieracium rigidum subsp. insuetum, also known as a rare species of the Hawkweed genus, is a type of flowering plant that is native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Hieracium rigidum subsp. insuetum are derived from its leaves and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. This plant is believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, anti-arthritic effects, and antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium rigidum subsp. insuetum are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness their medicinal properties.
